extra.reasoning:
The property (monotonicity) requires that flipping slurred_speech from false to true, with all other inputs unchanged, never lowers acuity. It is VIOLATED.

slurred_speech appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100), which requires all twelve of {focal_weakness, vision_loss, abdominal_pain, palpitations, leg_swelling, fever, sweating, hemoptysis, back_pain, syncope, dyspnea, slurred_speech} to be true and sets acuity := self_care (the lowest level). dizziness is not part of this conjunction.

Counterexample (compare two inputs differing only in slurred_speech):

BEFORE (slurred_speech = false, all other 11 conjunction vars true, dizziness false): interaction_drop does NOT fire because slurred_speech is false. The remaining rules fire; multiple priority-3 emergent rules apply (e.g., raise_syncope, raise_fever, raise_palpitations, raise_hemoptysis, raise_vision_loss), so the winning result is acuity = emergent (highest priority among firing rules is 3, all yielding emergent).

AFTER (slurred_speech = true, everything else identical): now the entire conjunction of interaction_drop is satisfied. With priority 100 it dominates all the priority-2 and priority-3 rules, setting acuity := self_care.

Thus acuity drops from emergent to self_care when slurred_speech is set true — a strict decrease in the enum order [self_care, routine, urgent, emergent]. This directly contradicts the monotonicity requirement. The witness_assignment given is the AFTER state (slurred_speech=true → self_care); its slurred_speech=false counterpart yields emergent, demonstrating the violation.
